name: Andy Ngo
subject: Help me bring Antifa to justice
body:
I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs

| |
---
| |
---
[](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck)
---
My fellow concerned American, I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs. And unless we do something to stop them today, I won't be the last. **[I'm asking you to donate $35 or more today to help me bring Antifa and its members who assaulted me to justice.](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck) ** **I was at Antifa's "ground zero" in Portland, Oregon during the summer of 2019.** This was before most Americans had ever heard of the Antifa movement, but they'd caused havoc and violence before. I went to an Antifa "protest" as a journalist to document and report on their "protest." As soon as I arrived, I feared for my safety. The mob was angry, and violence always follows Antifa. Most of them wore masks to prevent being identified when they commit crimes. Many had on helmets and carried riot gear. Some, I later learned, secretly had weapons like baseball bats, brass knuckles and even bricks. I was scared, but I had a job to do, so I pressed on. **People like you needed to know what was happening.** But I didn't go unnoticed for long. Somebody recognized me and bashed me on the back of my head in an ambush. "F*** you, Andy Ngo!" shouted voices around me. "Go home, fascist!" **Fists started flying at my face and head. Then came the projectiles.** Before I knew it, I was being repeatedly punched, kicked, and hit with objects. I tried to protect my head. I did not strike back as I knew that would only escalate the situation and put me in more danger. The thugs robbed me of my camera so that I wouldn't have evidence of the assault. What they did to me is unacceptable and criminal. And now they're trying to do the same to other innocent Americans like you. That's why I sued members of the Rose City Antifa organization to bring them to justice and send the loud clear message that they are NOT above the law. Now I need your help to hold their feet to the fire and make sure justice is served. [**Will you donate $35 or more to help me hold Antifa accountable?**](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck) This is bigger than me alone. After I got away from the mob, police finally showed up when I was in the hospital. But my attackers stole my evidence and nearly silenced me permanently with a traumatic brain injury. Later, cell phone footage from a bystander surfaced online. Thank goodness! The video captured part of the attack, clear as day. I hoped I'd get the justice I deserved. Wrong. To this day, six years later, the Portland Police have done nothing. **No charges. No arrests. No suspects questioned.** It's not much of a surprise, though. Radical politicians have long since bowed to the dangerous left-wing mobs that terrorize their streets. That's why the Center for American Liberty helped me file a lawsuit against my attackers. A judge ordered three of them to pay damages - but more than a year later and they have not paid a dime. So now the Center for American Liberty is helping me track them down and make them pay for nearly killing me and trampling upon the First Amendment. Unfortunately it's going to cost at least $20,000 to get justice. [**That's why I hope you'll join the fight by donating $35 or more to fund my efforts to hold Antifa and its members accountable.**](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck) We have to stop Antifa and make them face real, legal consequences for their destructive actions before they get to you and your family. Antifa wants the literal end of America -- and they'll stop at nothing to get it. Believe me when I say that's not an overstatement. Some members are avowed socialists or communists. But many are much worse - open violent anarchists who want to tear down civilization and remake it in their own twisted vision with no law and order. By any means necessary. **_Rioting. Destruction. Mayhem._** And they know, as long as they choose politically favored protests to co-opt, the media and politicians will never have the guts to denounce their violence or hold them accountable. They did that with BLM. They're doing it now against ICE. It's a sick strategy, but very smart - because so far, they've been successful. **For the most part, they've gotten off scot-free.** Our politicians and media are treating this terrorist group like peaceful protestors! As we saw in Los Angeles earlier this summer, their riots will only spread and intensify if Antifa continue to escape consequences. **So now I'm taking justice into my own hands - with the help of the talented lawyers at the Center for American Liberty.**

[**You can be a part of bringing Antifa to justice with a donation of $35 or more today.**](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck) Though I'm seeking justice for myself, the impact of this case could be so much larger. This is about standing up for our country when our own leaders won't. This is about saving America as we know it and keeping you and your family safe. And this is just the first blow we intend to deliver. By taking them to court for the attack against me we can prove they are NOT untouchable. **[Can I count on you to donate $35 or more to help me get justice from Antifa?](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck) ** Please don't wait - if we don't fight back and win now, **these violent thugs will be in EVERY city across America** , destroying everything and everyone in their path. Thank you in advance for your support.
---
| | Sincerely,
---
| | [](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkcuK2zAYhZ9G3slIv27xwoskTrJppmUK06XQ5Zej4kuQlZS-fQlMH2C2H-cc-Di_rvbq8pSX8c3N2Advy2ZvON1n9CUvo1tqTq6uvx9bzcECA0U5o6BfOd7EPnQI0ssGe240l8ZIpppbjwDYpRhBRAm7pHyKQvDEBWiFRvkm968tzkBzzhU3LVfMSOB-xzuvPXoiGcZc19KGddmwPF3NT_RldTG4rbYL1mbqb7XeNyL2BM4Ezm7BuNZ2yh5L_RtwqVjatYwEzkJpv4MOaRdiolIzQXcmCtqpJF2XnDIxEXF-1Nlu66MEJGI4DBf6fvogoF94xpgfMxHD6fpJgpvvLo8LEcNLhhnB7GG42ON5_83u3y7ff9r304f9safHw2elBiKG44H-l29KH-9_8jTlTRsi2Ti7PLVhnZv65TOePfwLAAD__9N1kck)
---
**Andy Ngo**
---
|

Today, we can make them face the consequences that left-wing politicians refuse to dole out. That's why the Center for American Liberty helped me file a lawsuit against my attackers. A judge ordered three of them to pay damages - but more than a year later and they have not paid a dime. So now the Center for American Liberty is helping me track them down and make them pay for nearly killing me and trampling upon the First Amendment. Unfortunately it's going to cost at least $20,000 to get justice. We have to stop Antifa and make them face real, legal consequences for their destructive actions before they get to you and your family. Antifa wants the literal end of America -- and they'll stop at nothing to get it. Believe me when I say that's not an overstatement. Some members are avowed socialists or communists. But many are much worse - open violent anarchists who want to tear down civilization and remake it in their own twisted vision with no law and order. By any means necessary. Rioting. Destruction. Mayhem. And they know, as long as they choose politically favored protests to co-opt, the media and politicians will never have the guts to denounce their violence or hold them accountable. They did that with BLM. They're doing it now against ICE. It's a sick strategy, but very smart - because so far, they've been successful. For the most part, they've gotten off scot-free. Our politicians and media are treating this terrorist group like peaceful protestors! As we saw in Los Angeles earlier this summer, their riots will only spread and intensify if Antifa continue to escape consequences. So now I'm taking justice into my own hands - with the help of the talented lawyers at the Center for American Liberty. Though I'm seeking justice for myself, the impact of this case could be so much larger. This is about standing up for our country when our own leaders won't. This is about saving America as we know it and keeping you and your family safe. And this is just the first blow we intend to deliver. By taking them to court for the attack against me we can prove they are NOT untouchable. Please don't wait - if we don't fight back and win now, these violent thugs will be in EVERY city across America , destroying everything and everyone in their path. Thank you in advance for your support. | | Sincerely, | | Andy Ngo | | | P.S. I know firsthand that America's troubles with Antifa aren't over -- they're just beginning**. Just look at what happened in Los Angeles earlier this summer. Today, we can make them face the consequences that left-wing politicians refuse to dole out. | | | The Center For American Liberty is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization fighting against growing anti-free speech and anti-civil liberties trends. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

One that could trigger a $12 trillion shift in global manufacturing.

But if you see it now, you’ll understand why some believe this could reshape America’s economy — and create once-in-a-lifetime gains for those who move early.

[>>>Watch what they caught — and what it really means](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=15b1f80894a04094afad05d78b6e0cac&_e=dgEqW6vHWvoHFCKaDJcYSgAtIjeTlnfWnWIEvpoxjV-4NAYagY0Tu3VOih_hA49zgtCAPoa5apqvxUEe7JQ48J4BjLtJg9cqbGOJdrIihK1DMri7ErtANhoBKOXu4aZ2X_07UMWli8j4n16D4XsUKg_97SPWsVETqdwGlEYLK0TdJ6moHHF0melHAxMSydziw2uMAvAvl-Wmmmt-FRCWbXZwB6o5GghDhJcnZ8eYvVcSpsgoNV61JsT9SW6VqiRyutC801z3wkJpu3k64RpuTKNDe29o8Nmm2xsTDKofkPjCktlr-Z5Vj5fDm8emwu2mg-AYj_TWzLYwgmvURQ5gxoXwUQMLnb0VoXqA-hO2cL33CJf0DE1t5Wj8V2kKjVuGORhyJOtxS2_CvGdF-TRAwZsnlVN0t8OJfQ7xJl37bhX5vtCNLnieROe9y1L5oeV2x0ENcmHxAO4HiNai4FsAzZ7Z_4HNdeejAShK4MGVkOwMXHi4S98f-CyeEuOyZ4eg4Gwy7E-pj-H3ExUux8IWVQj3E75bFBj-LI92bbd3UhJAIWaRapXfakIzDHZWZ5bJ)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. This isn’t about EVs. It isn’t about rockets. It’s about a brand-new technology reportedly under development at one of Elon Musk’s ventures... One that could trigger a $12 trillion shift in global manufacturing. But if you see it now, you’ll understand why some believe this could reshape America’s economy — and create once-in-a-lifetime gains for those who move early. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Atlantic Ave., Suite 200 Delray Beach, FL 33444 Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: The price of Krasner's latest catastrophe
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning, and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

[S](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=64a91e8d38&e=b7b3eddce2)[ubscribe to our daily email list ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=072e5bfec0&e=b7b3eddce2)and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1.[Ideology over justice — How Larry Krasner’s policies failed Kada Scott](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=dfb0c0feb9&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Ben Mannes_
Philadelphia District Attorney Larry Krasner is facing growing outrage following the brutal kidnapping and murder of 23-year-old Kada Scott — a crime many argue could have been prevented had his office competently handled earlier charges against the alleged killer, 21-year-old Keon King.

At a press briefing last week, Krasner attempted to shift responsibility for King’s release.

“The unfortunate reality,” Krasner told reporters, “is that some, but not all, of these judges don’t want you calling them in the middle of the night” to appeal bail decisions. It was a familiar refrain for a DA who has long blamed the system he swore to reform. But as court officials later made clear, Krasner’s story [doesn’t hold up](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=5944b03897&e=b7b3eddce2).

**Why It Matters**. Former Magistrate Judge Jim O’Brien dismissed Krasner’s claim that his office was deterred from appealing the $200,000 bail set by a magistrate because judges dislike middle-of-the-night calls.

“After bail is set, both the D.A.’s office and the defense have the right to an immediate appeal to the emergency standby judges,” O’Brien said. “They have a two-hour window to file that appeal... and the standby judge is available 24/7. I don’t know of a single judge who would punish the appellant by setting the bail higher or lower to discourage people from waking them up. His assertion is nonsense.”

That process is well established: the preliminary arraignment court operates 24 hours a day, every day of the year. If Krasner’s staff failed to pursue a bail modification, the responsibility lies squarely with his office — not with the judiciary.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=f9d2d3ebc2&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2.[When compassion means intervention — rethinking how we treat the severely mentally ill](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=9127ee920d&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Richard Kosich_
"I don’t even know the lady...why would somebody stab somebody for no reason?” That’s what a homeless ex-con can be[ heard](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=6261ab649f&e=b7b3eddce2) asking his sister during an August 28 jailhouse phone conversation they had while he was incarcerated. That career criminal is murder suspect Decarlos Brown Jr., who allegedly fatally stabbed 23-year old Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska in what police said was a random attack on a North Carolina light rail train last August 22. Brown’s family says he was a previously diagnosed schizophrenic who suffered from hallucinations and paranoia and was “hearing voices” prior to the attack. Brown was failed by the state, his younger sister added, saying that he was “not safe” to be released as he was a “high risk” and “not in his right mind.” Brown’s mother confirmed his chronic mental health struggles but also believed the murder could have been prevented with a successful mental health intervention while he was previously incarcerated. **Why It Matters.** It’s no secret that mental health issues and substance abuse are[ prevalent](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=5be40579dc&e=b7b3eddce2) among the homeless population. The [National Coalition for the Homeless](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=5178b3c3f5&e=b7b3eddce2), for example, reports that approximately 20-25 percent of the homeless population in the U.S. suffers from serious mental illness, compared to 6 percent of the general population. Here in Pennsylvania, the National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) estimates that[ one in five](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c0fb68a66c&e=b7b3eddce2) of the more than 12,500 people in the commonwealth who are without homes have a serious mental illness. It’s even more prevalent in Philadelphia; while people with serious mental illness make up just under three percent of the adult population in Philadelphia, an estimated [40 percent](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=02ed1ee770&e=b7b3eddce2) of the city’s homeless population, most of whom are chronically homeless, have such a condition. Pennsylvania’s state laws have been criticized for requiring probability that death, serious bodily injury, or serious physical debilitation will occur within 30 days to trigger an involuntary civil commitment, based exclusively on whether such harms have occurred within the prior 30 days, but without reference to an individual’s treatment history. **[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=15a79110bd&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Mark Schweiker: Pennsylvania’s energy future is bright and bipartisan](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=dbf942a671&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Becky Corbin: Cancer is cancer. Pennsylvanians must prioritize early screening.](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=7e26ffb484&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Christine Flowers: Judging the judges and there’s a lot to not like](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=bf80aacb4f&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Guy Ciarrocchi: Why won’t Democrats condemn violence?](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=2d7b222922&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Thom Nickels: Psychic to (Philadelphia) stars](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ef37422ba0&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Brandon Graham returns when the Eagles need him most](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=cc350677c6&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Paul Davis: Teens, terror, and social media](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=b097ecf2df&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Seth Higgins: People will demand order](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ba90ce88cd&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading**
Across the river in New Jersey, they’re having a governor’s race. If you watch the Eagles, you’ve probably seen the ads. The latest polls show Democrat Mikie Sherrill up five points over Republican Jack Ciattarelli, but Ryan King at the New York Post suggests that [this may actually be good news](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=51c8f946e8&e=b7b3eddce2) for the Republican.

The polls in this race four years ago were wrong — dramatically so. Could the same be true again? “Overall, Sherrill has a 4.1-point lead in the latest [RealClearPolitics aggregate](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=10f3a701eb&e=b7b3eddce2), which underestimated Ciattarelli’s by about 5 points during his 2021 gubernatorial bid.” Maybe the pollsters have refined their systems. Maybe they haven’t. Betting markets still [favor Sherrill](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4dfe830156&e=b7b3eddce2), but the pundits think this one will be closer than advertised.****
---
|
---
|
# **5\. We are so back.**
After last week’s offensive explosion and the return of Brandon Graham, are the Eagles back? Managing wide receivers — often the most temperamental and emotional of players — is always a fraught process, Mike Missanelli writes this week. Read [more about that and more](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=087bd2a0d2&e=b7b3eddce2) from him at On Pattison — a part of the Fideri News Network.
---
| [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4e88a47bc9&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
| [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a211439124&e=b7b3eddce2)

As always, gifts made to _Broad + Liberty_ are 100% tax deductible!

Pennsylvania’s state laws have been criticized for requiring probability that death, serious bodily injury, or serious physical debilitation will occur within 30 days to trigger an involuntary civil commitment, based exclusively on whether such harms have occurred within the prior 30 days, but without reference to an individual’s treatment history. | | 3\. Lightning Round | | 4\. What we're reading Across the river in New Jersey, they’re having a governor’s race. If you watch the Eagles, you’ve probably seen the ads. The latest polls show Democrat Mikie Sherrill up five points over Republican Jack Ciattarelli, but Ryan King at the New York Post suggests that for the Republican. The polls in this race four years ago were wrong — dramatically so. Could the same be true again? “Overall, Sherrill has a 4.1-point lead in the latest , which underestimated Ciattarelli’s by about 5 points during his 2021 gubernatorial bid.” Maybe the pollsters have refined their systems. Maybe they haven’t. Betting markets still , but the pundits think this one will be closer than advertised.** | | 5\. We are so back. After last week’s offensive explosion and the return of Brandon Graham, are the Eagles back? Managing wide receivers — often the most temperamental and emotional of players — is always a fraught process, Mike Missanelli writes this week. Read from him at On Pattison — a part of the Fideri News Network. | | | | Broad + Liberty is funded by readers like you. Your generous support ensures that Broad + Liberty can bring you stories and opinions that Pennsylvania’s mainstream media would rather leave unheard. Please consider making a contribution today. If you would like your gift to be used to help us cover a specific story or subject, please let us know in the contribution form or at mailto:editirs@broadandliberty.com. As always, gifts made to Broad + Liberty are 100% tax deductible! Thank you, dear reader, for your steadfast support of our independent, broad-minded brand of local journalism. We could not do this without you. With gratitude, — The Editors at Broad + Liberty | | "Support Broad + Liberty" | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2025 Broad + Liberty, All rights reserved. | [](https://substack.com/redirect/d1f95673-7a31-4e31-8225-6caec1f0ea9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I've recently been on the road a lot and have had meetings in Tennessee, South Dakota, Arkansas, and Washington DC. I also made a quick trip to Barranquilla, Colombia to give a keynote speech on navigating global uncertainty, and while in Washington, was on a panel discussing the US economy and how consumer behavior might change in the future.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c1ae340b-a433-46cd-90d6-585a68e6d2fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
My talk at the #CaribeBiz Forum in Barranquilla, Colombia on October 16, 2025.
As I've often said, travel is one of the best forms of education today, and I relish the opportunity to meet with journalists, politicians, business leaders, and academics while on the road. The exchange of ideas with people who have different perspectives on global and economic developments is one of my favorite ways to make sense of the world. Connecting the dots formed from different perspectives is, I believe, essential to understanding dynamics that appear chaotic or confusing.
* * *
## [US-Colombia Relations Deteriorate](https://substack.com/redirect/318af807-ec3d-4d5f-b6dc-ba5bf5deaaec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Oct 19
[](https://substack.com/redirect/318af807-ec3d-4d5f-b6dc-ba5bf5deaaec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I recently returned from a trip to Colombia where I had the chance to speak with the country's business and political leaders and share my thoughts on global trends, the ongoing trade war, and Americ...](https://substack.com/redirect/318af807-ec3d-4d5f-b6dc-ba5bf5deaaec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/318af807-ec3d-4d5f-b6dc-ba5bf5deaaec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Before I board a plane again next week, I wanted to reflect on some common themes that have emerged from my eclectic set of recent meetings. While this may sound like the beginning of a joke, it's not: What do entrepreneurs in Colombia, auto dealers in Washington DC, politicians from Tennessee, farmers in South Dakota, and corporate executives in Arkansas have in common?
While there is of course a lot a nuance beyond my summary, there are three topics that came up everywhere: (1) the escalating US-China War (the use of the word "war" is mine, as I wrote earlier), (2) the health of the US economy and implications for US interest rates and consumer confidence, and (3) artificial intelligence (AI). And as regular readers of my work know, I think these three dynamics are interrelated. **The tariffs and trade war with China will impact the US economy, AI is a domain of competition but also a deflationary force that replaces jobs and increases productivity, and of course, tariffs and AI will drive consumer confidence and impact the outlook for US interest rates.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Rjt2yBhYx9UO6jEzhEVxVkqQHVVmGuRw1iw4UrX_4Js?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=177090727)
Perhaps the most interesting topic that emerged from my conversations, however, was about how China's internal dynamics might impact its external relations. There's a rising interest in what's happening _**within**_ China and how President Xi is navigating his uncertainties. There are early signs of internal discord within the Middle Kingdom and it naturally leads to the question of how it might impact Beijing's actions vis-a-vis global conflicts and/or their approach to trade with America.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/27a4fed8-6b9c-436a-851c-81dc027831dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
(C) VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI, slide from presentation given in January 2025 at the Cayman Alternative Investment Summit (CAIS).
For those that haven't been following the story closely, China's [economy is plunging](https://substack.com/redirect/a413c198-3ef6-486e-881b-b982b95f624d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), its [currency depreciating](https://substack.com/redirect/ab192b5c-50c4-4a7f-b0d5-bf99af49ee87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [youth unemployment is skyrocketing](https://substack.com/redirect/56927f2c-9ef5-457c-b908-9c5053c1a99c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), its [domestic debt is ballooning](https://substack.com/redirect/9ff13321-05db-4297-a6b8-5dc14b71ecc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and as we all hear, the [trade conflict with the US is intensifying](https://substack.com/redirect/df6b22a8-8a3e-43d3-a40e-12217832f1bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). These internal problems have led to crackdowns on internal dissent, saber-rattling to generate nationalism, and CCP leadership efforts to solidify control. Last week, the Chinese Communist Party's ["Fourth Plenum"](https://substack.com/redirect/a39a6a4c-2a6d-4c06-a5c3-d499e3bd4e67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was the scene of what many have called a "military [purge](https://substack.com/redirect/5c9594f1-845d-4c0c-949e-496a5d863683?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" as President Xi sought to tighten his grip on power.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Rjt2yBhYx9UO6jEzhEVxVkqQHVVmGuRw1iw4UrX_4Js?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=177090727)
China's domestic discord is a topic that came up in conversations I had in Washington DC as well as in Colombia. Why's that? Because there is an emerging sense that Beijing may be retrenching to its immediate neighborhood while it works to get its own house in order. This could mean that Xi is pulling back (on the margin) from the far-flung influence empire it was building over the past few decades. The implications for conflicts in Latin America, and specifically Venezuela, could be material.
At the same time, the US has been refocusing on the Western Hemisphere. I've been commenting for years that the historical US attention on East-West relations with Asia and Europe is being replaced with a North-South focus (a modern-day version of the Monroe Doctrine, something I've called the Trump Doctrine). It's increasingly clear that the US is prioritizing the Western Hemisphere, and while some see this as isolationism, I have argued that this is in fact retrenchment.
* * *
## [America's Evolving Global Role](https://substack.com/redirect/db32a9fd-9df8-4046-b6b6-c0041af1408d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Feb 27
[](https://substack.com/redirect/db32a9fd-9df8-4046-b6b6-c0041af1408d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Since the day he descended an escalator to upend American politics, President Donald Trump has consistently delighted his supporters and confounded his critics. Seemingly on separate planets, these t...](https://substack.com/redirect/db32a9fd-9df8-4046-b6b6-c0041af1408d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/db32a9fd-9df8-4046-b6b6-c0041af1408d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Further, it's obvious to me that the Trump Administration isn't against interventionism altogether, it is just more selective as to where it is going to intervene. Recent [events](https://substack.com/redirect/de72b0ce-8c1d-46b7-8521-bffd29121426?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the Caribbean confirm this reality, and last week I [wrote](https://substack.com/redirect/318af807-ec3d-4d5f-b6dc-ba5bf5deaaec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the build-up of U.S. assets in the region, which includes a nuclear sub, cruise missile destroyers, fighter jets, spy planes, B-52 bombers, and 6,500 troops. Let's add the world's largest [aircraft carrier](https://substack.com/redirect/fba754d1-c5ae-4dda-a8ec-310190a626df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the list.
The US leaning into the Western Hemisphere while China retrenches will only hasten what I have referred to as the Global Split, a [bifurcation](https://substack.com/redirect/ba354f86-cde2-42c3-810b-f87694f299fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the world into two camps, East and West. The Americans and the Chinese are carving out two spheres of influence and two separate economic ecosystems are quickly evolving.
* * *
## [The Global Split Accelerates ](https://substack.com/redirect/ba354f86-cde2-42c3-810b-f87694f299fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Apr 26
[](https://substack.com/redirect/ba354f86-cde2-42c3-810b-f87694f299fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I have long been thought the world is in the process of bifurcating into two economic ecosystems, one led by America and the other by China. As is now obvious to the world, the split is accelerating ...](https://substack.com/redirect/ba354f86-cde2-42c3-810b-f87694f299fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/ba354f86-cde2-42c3-810b-f87694f299fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
The biggest implication of these observations is that countries (and therefore companies) need to pick a side. The days of seeking US legal or military protection while accepting Chinese investment dollars and maintaining close ties with Beijing are over. This also means that companies that rely on tools (software, equipment, AI tools) purchased from Chinese companies may suffer geopolitically inspired disruptions to service, support, or use of these tools.
Having a supply chain that runs through China is going to be increasingly risky for businesses focused on the US market, and may ultimately result in a higher cost of capital, higher labor costs, and less interest from forward-looking customers. Bottom line: It's time for leaders to pick; "neutrality" is not a option. As is often said in New Hampshire, the only things you find in the middle of the road are yellow stripes and dead skunks.

Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/9e357c75-3d31-4bf8-8945-5dc9bfb6755a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/4c4e52dc-cfa5-4c0a-8df3-bd4ca4c04cd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/584673c0-4945-4d75-b706-b3285374b4a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. clean_body: "Neutrality" is no longer an option in the US China War | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? "Neutrality" is no longer an option in the US China War | | | Oct 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- I've recently been on the road a lot and have had meetings in Tennessee, South Dakota, Arkansas, and Washington DC. I also made a quick trip to Barranquilla, Colombia to give a keynote speech on navigating global uncertainty, and while in Washington, was on a panel discussing the US economy and how consumer behavior might change in the future. | | ---|---|--- My talk at the #CaribeBiz Forum in Barranquilla, Colombia on October 16, 2025. As I've often said, travel is one of the best forms of education today, and I relish the opportunity to meet with journalists, politicians, business leaders, and academics while on the road. The exchange of ideas with people who have different perspectives on global and economic developments is one of my favorite ways to make sense of the world. Connecting the dots formed from different perspectives is, I believe, essential to understanding dynamics that appear chaotic or confusing. | | | | ---|---|--- | Oct 19 | Before I board a plane again next week, I wanted to reflect on some common themes that have emerged from my eclectic set of recent meetings. While this may sound like the beginning of a joke, it's not: What do entrepreneurs in Colombia, auto dealers in Washington DC, politicians from Tennessee, farmers in South Dakota, and corporate executives in Arkansas have in common? While there is of course a lot a nuance beyond my summary, there are three topics that came up everywhere: (1) the escalating US-China War (the use of the word "war" is mine, as I wrote earlier), (2) the health of the US economy and implications for US interest rates and consumer confidence, and (3) artificial intelligence (AI). And as regular readers of my work know, I think these three dynamics are interrelated. The tariffs and trade war with China will impact the US economy, AI is a domain of competition but also a deflationary force that replaces jobs and increases productivity, and of course, tariffs and AI will drive consumer confidence and impact the outlook for US interest rates. Perhaps the most interesting topic that emerged from my conversations, however, was about how China's internal dynamics might impact its external relations. There's a rising interest in what's happening within China and how President Xi is navigating his uncertainties. There are early signs of internal discord within the Middle Kingdom and it naturally leads to the question of how it might impact Beijing's actions vis-a-vis global conflicts and/or their approach to trade with America. | | ---|---|--- (C) VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI, slide from presentation given in January 2025 at the Cayman Alternative Investment Summit (CAIS). For those that haven't been following the story closely, China's , its , , its , and as we all hear, the . These internal problems have led to crackdowns on internal dissent, saber-rattling to generate nationalism, and CCP leadership efforts to solidify control. Last week, the Chinese Communist Party's was the scene of what many have called a "military " as President Xi sought to tighten his grip on power. China's domestic discord is a topic that came up in conversations I had in Washington DC as well as in Colombia. Why's that? Because there is an emerging sense that Beijing may be retrenching to its immediate neighborhood while it works to get its own house in order. This could mean that Xi is pulling back (on the margin) from the far-flung influence empire it was building over the past few decades. The implications for conflicts in Latin America, and specifically Venezuela, could be material. At the same time, the US has been refocusing on the Western Hemisphere. I've been commenting for years that the historical US attention on East-West relations with Asia and Europe is being replaced with a North-South focus (a modern-day version of the Monroe Doctrine, something I've called the Trump Doctrine). It's increasingly clear that the US is prioritizing the Western Hemisphere, and while some see this as isolationism, I have argued that this is in fact retrenchment. | | | | ---|---|--- | Feb 27 | Further, it's obvious to me that the Trump Administration isn't against interventionism altogether, it is just more selective as to where it is going to intervene. Recent in the Caribbean confirm this reality, and last week I about the build-up of U.S. assets in the region, which includes a nuclear sub, cruise missile destroyers, fighter jets, spy planes, B-52 bombers, and 6,500 troops. Let's add the world's largest to the list. The US leaning into the Western Hemisphere while China retrenches will only hasten what I have referred to as the Global Split, a of the world into two camps, East and West. The Americans and the Chinese are carving out two spheres of influence and two separate economic ecosystems are quickly evolving. | | | | ---|---|--- | Apr 26 | The biggest implication of these observations is that countries (and therefore companies) need to pick a side. The days of seeking US legal or military protection while accepting Chinese investment dollars and maintaining close ties with Beijing are over. This also means that companies that rely on tools (software, equipment, AI tools) purchased from Chinese companies may suffer geopolitically inspired disruptions to service, support, or use of these tools. Having a supply chain that runs through China is going to be increasingly risky for businesses focused on the US market, and may ultimately result in a higher cost of capital, higher labor costs, and less interest from forward-looking customers. Bottom line: It's time for leaders to pick; "neutrality" is not a option. As is often said in New Hampshire, the only things you find in the middle of the road are yellow stripes and dead skunks. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . body:
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1919%2E791427%2EV6OCwS)
Friend, we’re reaching out today because, despite Trump’s government shutdown nearing the four week mark, we still need to make sure our fundraising for our campaign for reelection stays on track.
So, we’re hoping you can chip in $10 to Ilhan Omar today and help her continue to stand strong for our progressive values.
$10 because we need to build a nationwide, grassroots movement that can oppose Trump’s authoritarian rule and far-right agenda.
$10, because unlike other members of Congress, Ilhan has never taken a dime from corporate Super PACs or far-right mega-donors to fund this campaign.
$10 because that means she needs a lot of donations from folks like you, to build the warchest this campaign needs to win what could be an extremely tough primary.
Listen, our fundraising has been lagging well behind our goals during this shutdown. We understand it’s a tough time for some to chip in. We also know $10 doesn’t sound like much in the face of AIPAC, billionaire oligarchs, and right-wing attack ads.
But trust us: **it all adds up.** We have seen time and time again that a grassroots funded campaign can beat anyone if we stay organized. If you can dig deep today, it would make a huge difference.
**[Chip in your $10 to Ilhan Omar right now and join the thousands of others who are doing the same. We are just five days away from our October deadline and we just can’t afford to end the month behind our goals.](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-oct25.q9Ch2B&refcode=em251026-1-180&t=2&akid=1919%2E791427%2EV6OCwS)**

Consider yourself warned: **_if the Fed Cuts rates on 10/29, gold is going higher._**
The signs were clear before the last surge, and those who acted watched gold break through record highs while the dollar stumbled.
Now, it’s happening again.
The same setup that sent gold soaring is returning... only this time, the move could be even bigger.

* Interest rates are cornering the economy
* The dollar’s purchasing power continues to slide
* Central banks are buying gold at the fastest pace in decades

clean_body: Trump’s new policies could spark an even bigger gold surge. Dear Reader, Consider yourself warned: if the Fed Cuts rates on 10/29, gold is going higher. The signs were clear before the last surge, and those who acted watched gold break through record highs while the dollar stumbled. Now, it’s happening again. The same setup that sent gold soaring is returning... only this time, the move could be even bigger. Behind the headlines, Trump’s policy shift and the Fed’s losing battle with inflation are creating a perfect storm: Interest rates are cornering the economy The dollar’s purchasing power continues to slide Central banks are buying gold at the fastest pace in decades This isn’t speculation: it’s history repeating itself. And those who wait will watch the next breakout from the sidelines. We’ve already seen what happens when the Fed loses control. Now the world is positioning for the next phase, and the smart money is moving into physical gold and silver. Before the next surge begins, you still have time to secure your position. That’s why we’re offering you our Free Wealth Protection Kit — a step-by-step guide showing you exactly how to: ✅ Shield your savings from the falling dollar ✅ Legally hold gold and silver inside your IRA or 401(k) ✅ Position before the next move (not after it’s already begun) You’ve seen the proof. Gold has already broken out once. Don’t wait until the next wave leaves you behind. Secure your future today. Stay protected, \- The Noble Gold Investments Team P.S. Gold’s surge was just the opening act. The next wave could redefine the dollar and your financial future. Act now to protect your savings:

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I get it
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
I know my purpose. For my entire life, I’ve served this country. After my family, my purpose has been to defend our Constitution, our democracy, our people — and to do the most good I can for them.
#

Team —
There have been times in my life when it was hard to see the way forward. When it feels like you’ve got the weight of the world on your shoulders. When hope was hard to come by.
It's not easy. I get it.
A pretty smart guy named Viktor Frankl had some thoughts on how you keep going through tough times. “Those who have a ‘why’ to live, can bear with almost any ‘how.’”
You have to find a purpose, and that purpose often centers on love. Love for your family, for your friends, for your ideals, for your country. And sometimes that purpose is just to keep going, no matter what.
I know my purpose. For my entire life, I’ve served this country. After my family, my purpose has been to defend our Constitution, our democracy, our people — and to do the most good I can for them.
This team has been a huge part of that, and I can’t thank you enough. And you can be sure: I am not going to quit. I am not going to be quiet. I am going to keep standing up and doing what I think is right.
But, just as always, I can’t do this work alone.
Now, if any of that sounds like something you’d like to be a part of, I’ve got a humble request for you:
[**We have some incredibly powerful people lining up against us. To keep going, I’m asking you to make a donation to my campaign.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-oct25/.P901v0&refcode=em251026-1-8&t=1&akid=6227%2E6688874%2EzKiXyD)

clean_body: | | | | == | == | 9 Days To Go | | | Please ask friends to vote for Winsome Sears, John Reid, Jason Miyares, and the rest of the Republican ticket. | GET OUT THE VOTE VICTORY BUS TOUR | | | Team, Join us Monday to kickoff our Get Out The Vote Bus Tour with the John Fredericks Radio Show. Please bring friends, pick up signs, get breakfast, and help us get out the vote. Details follow: | County Line Mini Mall & Cafe 20677 Lynchburg Hwy, Forest, Va MONDAY Oct 27th 6 am - 10 am Coffee and Muffins will be served The John Fredericks Radio Show Broadcasting Live!! We will have yard signs and 4X8 signs. If you have a good highway spot in the 5th, email your name and address to john@mcguireva.com | | | Re-districting Nonsense | | == | A few years ago, Democrats put a ton of effort into establishing a non partisan re-districting commission so politicians could not gerrymander Virginia districts. Hypocrisy: Now Democrats want to eliminate the commission and gerrymander Virginia districts to keep Winsome off the campaign trail and take away your voice. "Democrats dragged us into a redistricting battle-using your tax dollars to pull me off the campaign trail." - Winsome Sears == | More Shutdown Nonsense | | | Democrat/ Schumer Shutdown Update: We have three democrat senators and we still need five more democrat senators to end the shutdown. Can you believe Schumer helped write the CR, he voted for it 13 times, and now he's voting no because he's afraid of AOC? You may know, I'm not taking a paycheck during the shutdown. But my team and I are still on the road working non stop to help folks all over the district. They are calling this the "Schumer Pain Game shutdown" because the political games the Democrats are playing are causing people to suffer. Please call your US Senators and ask them to stop putting politics over We The People and to vote to re-open the government. | | | Virginia Elections | "Did Spanberger learn to lie in the CIA?" == | Spanberger is no moderate, get facts here: == | Please Vote Ann Parker Campbell School Board | Vote Ann Parker for ==. Ann is also endorsed by my friend Attorney General Jason Miyares. I met Ann Parker in 2021 when she visited my office with members of Concerned Women for America. Ann is an advocate for conservative values, and increased opportunities for the students in Campbell County Schools and across the Commonwealth. Re-Elect Ann Parker, she will continue to fight for Virginia and our children. Thank you Ann Parker and Lynn Tucker for your Christian faith and all you do to help us get solid reliable Republican conservatives elected! | Please Vote David Christian Buckingham School Board | | | My friend and fellow Christian, David Christian is a lifelong Buckingham County resident and a former School Board member. He took our schools from bottom 5% to full accreditation, cut waste & raised teacher pay. He'll fight for: • Parents' voice in schools • Discipline & safe classrooms • Focus on academics, not politics • Competitive teacher pay Proven business owner & Pharmacy Director, David is ready to bring common sense back to School Board District 1. VOTE DAVID! | Thank You Goochland Tea Party and Others for Helping the Entire Republican Ticket | | | Thank you Goochland Tea Party, Delegate David Owen, and others for helping get out the vote. Stop by every Saturday weather permitting from 10am to 3pm across from the Food Lion on 30 Broad Street Rd Manakin Sabot, VA to get a sign, candidate info, Trump gear and Turning Point gear, and more. Please tell a friend. | God bless America! Please ask friends to vote Republican early. Take care, please tell a friend, and I look forward to seeing you around the district! == == == == | For God and Country, | Congressman John McGuire VA 05 Soli Deo Gloria Jesus is my Savior Husband Father Navy SEAL Business Owner -Trump Endorsed -NRA Endorsed -Susan B. Anthony's List Endorsed -RJC Endorsed -Mark Levin Endorsed Pro-Life Pro-Gun Pro-Israel American Legion Member VFW (Life Member) NRA (Life Member) Highest Award for Conservatism (ACU/ CPAC) Family Foundation 100 VCDL 100 (Life Member) America First The Spirit of Virginia == our Facebook Page Twitter: @JohnMcGuire4VA Instagram: JohnMcGuire4VA == == john@mcguireva.com www.mcguireva.com | | | | == | | == | == | == == == | Paid for and Authorized by McGuire for Virginia | | | | McGuire for Virginia | PO Box 242 | Manakin Sabot, VA 23103 US | | | | | | |

I'm Winsome Earle-Sears, and I've held many titles in my life. Each one hasn't been a cakewalk, but I like a challenge. I've tried to show my daughters that what you can accomplish is not decided by the color of your skin or where you were born.Sure, a silver spoon would've made it easier, but I've found a helping hand from your community means so much more. _**[That’s why I’m reaching out to you asking for a helping hand to propel me to a win in my race for Governor.](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=d0c6d1139eed460c808ec343981dbbc3&_e=iaezOfKLIYfP8STZdTFBwqKgilbsbG_Mhxji3UsZ6f6P9wkAzXjaPbvGNpFY5STLVh6whVLHoWMKvlOKwWf_DbvJat9oTEmHjFt_p5meDP-P0LjZq9GTTPN0bfjlDq9cPKhTuJgREyw3-4yuEkSrP4mEFvwnIq_G-tHdEoPjTqx4Jmu-k31iVpJkVxicNNaPKnTsCdCFOSStTuTBo6GAL4bKlkJKsJZ80qqZahVbzh9oo8bq7GIRHv5aeLvjYC0QIOI0eQORmkz8ViNi15pGbiGU0D-E8ahSB9I3e43nRRNI4F3drcxXeNtqcnAKNBMEiHAFS_NoJfajSfZXliyWZo3TdRCcuOuiTruJScp3BHihF_AyXmE_gnSNcDMOt1MB9Oc0f_o4hGdo2rXAVxF3obqKOxOVKIyxa5rAzxdx_JLRZXOlpfQukzL45-Laev7sR6cVbKJ_i9V4CLi9AtGtFU2Xn8Svc70Abo3ZJyOUge269p9meosReiWqIZJSEF3tirk31e_OL_WVVsYgriHkce4EpM27WZhrE_Vm8B2beYcedV1N5YyXoPbBVFXJOjJbl4vR1BH3ZL5joCqvPJFl9ZFxE1QMJgJs2tAR-ACHrvHs-AqpzYDAcz8hhXgFPcjovY7jEWJr9ornD_CCXC0pe9xzkzucTp2-SjSLQHgwIFyx3gDb3aZu2ht_YsoHNWQlG6PBVWcgN1zroA71CMYt-Wyeh-geQG-6VZ4azdWAaVhZz0cuckW5E7vp5ysKJQgFC6P4XU-0rJ7oDPP9yDIPP-2DkEbMRTJQLu4_HG-HbgBBTG9INONVT4wDOrDcrKP7lQp493LFw2Qp7vWNndfeR1P1_ooDcTTB34gHO3-j-xUkduH9kXs8jWjVR5UztQNb)**_ This will be another challenge, but with you on my side, I know I can do this.
