Goldstein For Congress - Please Subscribe and also visit us at www.goldsteinforcongress.com
* * *
# [Defeating Mamdani and Preserving the Property Rights of NYC Co-Op and Condominium Owners](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=177194865&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc3MTk0ODY1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjE1MDA4MzAsImV4cCI6MTc2NDA5MjgzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ILct0EWW5SWCn_h-ReUgo5aQlPihcJfy4LIVxaneccg)
### How The Affordable Housing Proposed by Connecticut Democrats Will Do the Same to Connecticut
| | [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct) and [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81)
---
| Oct 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)| | [](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81)
---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=177194865&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc3MTk0ODY1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MTUwMDgzMCwiZXhwIjoxNzY0MDkyODMwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTQ1NDg1MyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.QVZdjOYhFvN9S4yQCKNjWvWwkVW9Bayi7H5ZDj6DZLo&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

SAVE NYC is a pollical organization (Founded by Betsy McCaughey former NYS Lieutenant Governor and Ray Kelly former NYC Police Commissioner) for the purpose of stopping the election of Socialist NYC Mayoral Candidate Zohran Mamdani and stopping Socialist agenda of destroying private property rights in NYC. **Save NYC is focused on highlighting the devastation of Local Law 97.**
**What is Local Law 97 and what are its Consequences?**
Thanks for reading The Goldstein Substack! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..C2Y2Mg8vs65GHqfni7rgW8gzykxzFx0_AvgyiDSr5QA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=177194865)
Under Local Law 97, which takes effect over the next two years, apartment buildings including Co-Ops and Condominiums will have to convert from natural gas or oil to electric heat. **This is at their own expense.** The implementation requires a gut of building-wide heating from steam (from Con-Ed), diesel and natural gas, and the installation of costly heat pumps run by electricity. The only place for these units is a building roof and who knows if their roofs can withstand these units without structural upgrades. The result is **Electric Heat for All** with this law.
The costs including conversion and higher monthly heating bills will make living in their Co-Ops and Condos unaffordable for many New Yorkers. **If you think Electric AC is Bad, double or triple that cost for Electric Heat.**
**What is the Result of this Draconian Law?**
Buildings and owners faced with assessments that they cannot afford, will be forced to sell or abandon their units which will lead to a collapse of these markets, destruction of owner equity and force these former apartment owners to leave NYC. **Most of these buildings have a 3 month reserve for rainy day funds, but this is well beyond what any building could temporarily withstand with no end in sight to recoup these upgrade costs with unbearable assessments, devastation in retrofits and remediations for lead and asbestos common in many of these older buildings.**
Mamdani, a man who has never proposed any legislation, fully supports the rigorous enforcement of Local Law 97. Cuomo, a man who at least gets how building's operate, will delay enforcement and possibly more and Curtis Sliwa fully supports the elimination of this law.
_**The Cost of Electric Heat?**_
Electric heat is also much more expensive than either oil or natural gas. One gallon of heating oil generates as much heat as 40.6 KWH of electricity. The average cost per gallon of heating oil in NYC is $3.90 and the average per KWH cost is $0.31. Doing the math 40.6 KWH, which would provide as much heat as one gallon of heating oil would cost $12.59 to produce as much heat as a $3.90 gallon of heating oil.
One therm of natural gas generates as much heat as 29.3KWH. The average cost per therm in NYC is about $2.00 that is because there is an initial monthly charge in addition to a higher per therm charge for the first 43.9 therms and then a lower charge thereafter. It averages out to about $2.00 per therm. Doing the math 29.3 KWH which would provide as much heat as one therm would cost $9.08 to produce as much heat as $2.00 of natural gas.
Electric heat costs about 3 times as much as heating oil and 4.5 times as much as natural gas. Converting to electric heat would result in huge increases in heating costs with limited environmental impact. The electricity generated to produce electric heat is mostly done by burning fossil fuels.
_**SAVE NYC estimates that the conversion assessment and the increase in energy costs would increase monthly maintenance by about $1,000 per month and possibly more. With increased demand an no new supply electric rates would go higher and so would the monthly increase.**_
SAVE NYC correctly believes that many middle class and retired New Yorkers could not afford the $1,000 plus monthly increase in their living costs and would be forced to sell their apartments. This could easily lead to a huge glut of apartments for sale leading to drastic price reductions and or owners not being able to pay their maintenance and forcing bankruptcies of Co-Ops and the inability of Condos to collect the common charges. The market could collapse, apartment owners would not only loose their equity but also their ability to afford to live in their units.
They would have to find a new place to live, most likely out of NYC.
_**Mandani 's Socialist Dream of Eliminating the Ownership of Private Property**_
Rigorous enforcement of Local Law 97 would collapse the Co-Op and Condo market and accomplish Mamdani's dream, the elimination of private property rights in NYC. All of this would be done in the name of a bogus green energy scam. Fossil fuel burned elsewhere would produce the electricity to run these alleged "green energy buildings".
Once buildings go bankrupt, NYC would take them over and turn then into homeless housing. The homeless would have a place to live and the original homeowners would become homeless. That is the Mamdani dream.
New York voters must be made aware of the consequence of a $1,000 per month rent increase combined with significant higher electric bills because they would be paying for their own heat at over triple the price of their fossil fuel heat.
_**Misguided Ideology**_
Local Law 97 does not reduce greenhouse gas emission. All it does is make life less affordable with disastrous economic consequences and the electricity needed is still generated by Fossil Fuels.
**HOW DO YOU Protect NYC from the Destructive Policies of Mamdani?**
_Turnout is the Winning Strategy_
Voter turnout in NYC Mayoral Elections is low compared to the turnout in presidential election years. Since 2000 the average turnout in NYC for a presidential election is about 60% of the voters. Voter turnout in NYC Mayoral elections have been steadily dropping from 41% in 2000 to 23% in 2021. This is the key to the Socialist victory. The Socialist vote and the rest mostly stay home and the socialist win.
**Voter apathy is the death of democracy.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b7b74d7a-8116-4c1c-9313-700c237fe700?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is the problem and the solution. The Socialists will be energized and they will turnout. Unless there is strong turnout by everyday New Yorkers who will be negatively impacted by bad Socialist policies the Socialists will win.
**Mamdani will be defeated if average hard working New Yorkers go to the polls in large numbers and vote for anyone but Mamdani.**
If voter turnout is consistent with prior years Mamdani wins. If voter turnout is 45% then both Sliwa and Cuomo will outperform Mamdani. The math is simple if voters are made aware of the consequence of a Mamdani victory more everyday New Yorkers will turn out and vote.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d2217d20-3899-4f42-8559-1d232c5fcd9d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
That is why every Co-Op or Condo owner must wake up, vote and preserve their property right and home equity. Everyone who is opposed to Democrat Antisemitism most vote for someone other than Mamdani. Every New Yorker who is against higher taxes, defunding the police and government takeover of whatever they can get away with must step up and vote for someone other than Mamdani.
**Mamdani 's Socialism is Contagious - CONNECTICUT IS NEXT**
During the Pandemic, Connecticut Democrat leaders followed the lead of NYC Democrat leaders and let COVID infected individuals to be admitted to nursing homes. **Thousands died and on a per capita basis more people died in Connecticut Nursing Homes compared to NYC Nursing Home Covid deaths.**
It is never a good ideal to follow the leader and copy the mistake of NYC.
**Connecticut Version of Destroying Private Property Rights and Equity by Destroying Single Family Zoning**
Connecticut's Socialist policies will destroy middle class housing. It is not through the electric heat mandates but through the _**destruction of single family neighborhood by allowing under the pretext of affordable housing the building of multifamily housing units in single family zones.**_
_**Connecticut homeowners home equity and the right to live in single family zones is being attacked by the Connecticut Democrats under the guise of affordable housing.**_
When a one family neighborhood is converted into a mixed-use neighborhood with apartment buildings next to single family homes and even commercial establishments, the value of the home declines. The homeowner who purchased their home with the expectation that they would be living in a single-family neighborhood does not get the value of what they bargained for.
The multi-family apartment buildings will be rentals and renters will replace homeowners. Renters are less invested in the local landscape. They are deprived of the potential to build equity in their residence. This is a victory toward realizing the Socialist plan of eliminating private owned homes.
_**Municipal Elections in Connecticut Count**_
In Connecticut, municipal elections are underway. **THE ONLY WAY TO STOP THE DESTRUCTION OF THE SINGLE FAMILY NEIGHBORHOODS IS TO VOTE FOR LOCAL CANDIDATES WHO ARE OPPOSED TO THE DESTRUCTION OF THE SINGLE FAMILY NEIGHBORHOOD.** This will send a message to the Democrat State Legislature whose members face re-election next year.
Voters are fed up with all these mandates and want local control. They, not Hartford should decide the future of their towns.
If you want to create affordable housing and rehabilitate destroyed neighborhoods then support our 21st Century version of the Homestead Act.
Just as in NYC in Connecticut you vote counts and if you vote you will be protecting the "American Dream".
Early voting has started, and election day is next week.

Mamdani 's Socialism is Contagious - CONNECTICUT IS NEXT During the Pandemic, Connecticut Democrat leaders followed the lead of NYC Democrat leaders and let COVID infected individuals to be admitted to nursing homes. Thousands died and on a per capita basis more people died in Connecticut Nursing Homes compared to NYC Nursing Home Covid deaths. It is never a good ideal to follow the leader and copy the mistake of NYC. Connecticut Version of Destroying Private Property Rights and Equity by Destroying Single Family Zoning Connecticut's Socialist policies will destroy middle class housing. It is not through the electric heat mandates but through the destruction of single family neighborhood by allowing under the pretext of affordable housing the building of multifamily housing units in single family zones. Connecticut homeowners home equity and the right to live in single family zones is being attacked by the Connecticut Democrats under the guise of affordable housing. When a one family neighborhood is converted into a mixed-use neighborhood with apartment buildings next to single family homes and even commercial establishments, the value of the home declines. The homeowner who purchased their home with the expectation that they would be living in a single-family neighborhood does not get the value of what they bargained for. The multi-family apartment buildings will be rentals and renters will replace homeowners. Renters are less invested in the local landscape. They are deprived of the potential to build equity in their residence. This is a victory toward realizing the Socialist plan of eliminating private owned homes. Municipal Elections in Connecticut Count In Connecticut, municipal elections are underway. THE ONLY WAY TO STOP THE DESTRUCTION OF THE SINGLE FAMILY NEIGHBORHOODS IS TO VOTE FOR LOCAL CANDIDATES WHO ARE OPPOSED TO THE DESTRUCTION OF THE SINGLE FAMILY NEIGHBORHOOD.** This will send a message to the Democrat State Legislature whose members face re-election next year. Voters are fed up with all these mandates and want local control. They, not Hartford should decide the future of their towns. If you want to create affordable housing and rehabilitate destroyed neighborhoods then support our 21st Century version of the Homestead Act. Just as in NYC in Connecticut you vote counts and if you vote you will be protecting the "American Dream". Early voting has started, and election day is next week. name: Voter Action Project
subject: We can't let them take away vote-by-mail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Democracy Docket: With New Order, Trump Tries to Kill Off Vote By Mail [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_1&amount=25]
We're going to keep hammering this home, Team, because the mainstream media continues to ignore this executive order and how it will change our elections forever.
President Donald Trump's massive anti-voting executive order has sent lawmakers, election officials, and voting rights advocates into a frenzy. One provision focuses on a goal that for years has been a borderline obsession for MAGA: Cracking down on voting by mail.
For background: After every election in which he's been a candidate or in power, Trump has raged over the use of mail-in ballots in general and ballot deadlines in particular . He's tried to claim fraud for every single ballot counted after Election Day.
So of course, this order directs Attorney General Pam Bondi to “take all necessary action” against any states that count absentee or mail-in ballots received after Election Day.
This order makes it clear that the MAGA crew will do anything they can to codify their power permanently and silence the will of the people by suppressing and eliminating the votes we cast.
We've got to keep fighting and work to protect our elections and our fundamental right to vote. Step up with $15 or whatever you can today and help us defend vote by mail across the country >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_1&amount=15]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_1&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_1&amount=35]
We all know that mail-in voting laws are about ballot accessibility, making it easier for voters to participate in our elections . Elderly voters, the disabled, our military service members -- they all vote by mail.
Mail-in voting has happened dating back to the Civil War. It's always been safe and secure, mail-in voting fraud is supremely rare. States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. It's been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic.
Our partners at VoteAmerica said it best: “ There is no good-faith reason to limit vote-by-mail access, reject ballots without postmarks, or refuse to count ballots that arrive after Election Day. If the administration moves forward with these proposals, they will effectively disenfranchise older voters, rural voters, and military voters. ”
Opponents of voting rights have proven they'll go to any length necessary to stop people from voting and make sure that they stay in power. Will you chip in $15 or whatever you can to fight their unending desire for control and protect mail-in voting? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_1&amount=15]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=102625_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=102625_1&amount=20]

name: Fire MAGA
subject: Boebert gets paid while federal workers miss 2nd paycheck
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
She’s playing politics with lives, families across Colorado are cutting back — making impossible choices at the kitchen table about what they can afford this month.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pFiH7VXC5yTLP4kJ2vdtkgdY05YVmIHlDubxgAtz42uTylg1M3yuqGWT37t7al7GXJBdlYTBBhYBUIUuq3LCGCN5BLyASzzHjRcF0ShEKBg7h-vynuC2j9XIT0CxwrmZnrKV6YwFIwvJiUMswD-i_OxjBrTQ8ESgI9AnuD758ycmhpTsvV1d3EayQfE4pawHvXKY-YVr2WiGrVGPchUoPnfa-AHY01y5&c=f00evyL9qIwMNVbvte-y6JkQPLBWiRHk40NTFRF6JAQA1jyB8Ly6eA==&ch=utfaqrDsyZ5lUbtjekGi6ip5tbKE09IGB5C2xHAElroi7K-UGNANag==)
---
|

Peter,
Some of us earn our paychecks, others cash them.
I worked at the AFL-CIO, helping give voice to the fight for working people -- the nurses pulling doubles, the teachers buying their own supplies, the truck drivers hauling through snow to keep Colorado moving. People who earn what they take home.
Lauren Boebert? She cashes a taxpayer-funded check while federal workers aren't getting theirs--shutting down the government, jacking up health care premiums by up to 306%, and driving rural hospitals to the edge of collapse.
That's not work. That's stealing from the people who do.
Because while she's playing politics with people's lives, families across Colorado are cutting back -- making impossible choices at the kitchen table about what they can afford this month.
This campaign isn't about politics, it's about power. And it's time working people take it back.
Lauren Boebert can be beaten. She's in Congress because of less than 546 votes. In 2024 when we went head to head, our campaign had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district. Now I'm back to finish the job, 2026 is the year we fire Boebert.
[Small dollar donations add up. Can I count on you to pitch in $10 or whatever you can spare monthly to help us retire Lauren Boebert?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pFiH7VXC5yTLP4kJ2vdtkgdY05YVmIHlDubxgAtz42uTylg1M3yuqGWT37t7al7GXJBdlYTBBhYBUIUuq3LCGCN5BLyASzzHjRcF0ShEKBg7h-vynuC2j9XIT0CxwrmZnrKV6YwFIwvJiUMswD-i_OxjBrTQ8ESgI9AnuD758ycmhpTsvV1d3EayQfE4pawHvXKY-YVr2WjpM0X_Ngm_djWUMot9qP17&c=f00evyL9qIwMNVbvte-y6JkQPLBWiRHk40NTFRF6JAQA1jyB8Ly6eA==&ch=utfaqrDsyZ5lUbtjekGi6ip5tbKE09IGB5C2xHAElroi7K-UGNANag==)

clean_body: She’s playing politics with lives, families across Colorado are cutting back — making impossible choices at the kitchen table about what they can afford this month. | | | | | == | Peter, Some of us earn our paychecks, others cash them. I worked at the AFL-CIO, helping give voice to the fight for working people -- the nurses pulling doubles, the teachers buying their own supplies, the truck drivers hauling through snow to keep Colorado moving. People who earn what they take home. Lauren Boebert? She cashes a taxpayer-funded check while federal workers aren't getting theirs--shutting down the government, jacking up health care premiums by up to 306%, and driving rural hospitals to the edge of collapse. That's not work. That's stealing from the people who do. Because while she's playing politics with people's lives, families across Colorado are cutting back -- making impossible choices at the kitchen table about what they can afford this month. This campaign isn't about politics, it's about power. And it's time working people take it back. Lauren Boebert can be beaten. She's in Congress because of less than 546 votes. In 2024 when we went head to head, our campaign had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district. Now I'm back to finish the job, 2026 is the year we fire Boebert. == | | == | == | == | In solidarity, Trisha Calvarese Democrat for CO-04 | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | | | | | | |

We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8ed678b7-283c-4722-871c-1b70b74bd426/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| 🚨 Peter, this isn’t the news we wanted to share. Republican John Sununu and Democrat Chris Pappas are virtually tied in the latest poll for New Hampshire’s Senate seat. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6c58a69e-6f29-4b61-a5a1-78e862e83112/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| [Help us stay ahead by splitting a donation of whatever you can between Chris Pappas and Blue Amp Action today »](https://default.salsalabs.org/T08c7e521-bafa-4e48-8647-635cf70fef2a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Reminder: New Hampshire IS a purple state. Democrats can only take back the Senate majority if we defend this open seat, and Republicans know it. They’re going to throw everything they can into defeating our grassroots movement to elect Chris. But Chris Pappas knows how to win tough races, and he’ll take on anyone to do what’s best for Granite Staters. Sununu and the GOP will have the backing of corporate PACs and their megadonors, so we need to keep building our strong people-powered campaign to fight back. [Can you split a donation between Chris and Blue Amp Action to help us defend this seat for Democrats and Granite Staters? $15, $50, or even just $5 will help power our campaign.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0d094f97-0dec-4955-8b17-cde0d5abd4b1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T76073c5d-dfb9-48eb-a8b9-caac921b9180/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

In his short time as Speaker, he’s:

[**So we’re collecting 15,000 signatures from your city to DEMAND Congress fire Mike Johnson IMMEDIATELY!! But we just checked, and we’re still missing your signature, friend. **](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69630?t=8&akid=23547%2E4449941%2ERgkGN5)

Friend – I need to say thank you. I can’t tell you what it means to know that people like you have my back. I’m spending every single day working to do what I can to stop the Trump MAGA agenda. I’m fighting for healthcare funding for millions of Americans. I’m making every effort possible to stop GOP cuts that every day Delawareans and Americans rely on. _But I will also be honest: It’s an uphill battle with Trump in the White House and GOP control of Congress._ But that doesn’t mean the work we put in right now is for nothing, friend. If anything, it’s that much more important that we double down in our efforts and do everything we can to chip away at the cuts and destructiveness of this current Administration. My team is hoping to raise $5,500 from this email to build the momentum we need to take back the Senate in 2026. [**_Can you help us? (If everyone reading this gave just $3, we’d hit that goal in no time!)_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118045792/578476055/-486912179?amount=3&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.10.26_Gratitude_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_RS-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTBmMDg0Yi05ZGIyLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kX23Oy-wlvGxiAThMdISYD4Qs51mdBOV7tUT_calAGg=) | | [**DONATE »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118045793/578476056/-486912179?amount=3&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.10.26_Gratitude_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_RS-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTBmMDg0Yi05ZGIyLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kX23Oy-wlvGxiAThMdISYD4Qs51mdBOV7tUT_calAGg=)
---
I always said that the best of the American people comes out in the toughest of times.
These are tough times, friend.
But I don’t plan to back down from the fight. Do you?

# [Is Private Credit's "Fender Bender" about to become a major pileup?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4720068&post_id=176858400&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc2ODU4NDAwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjE1MDU3NjgsImV4cCI6MTc2NDA5Nzc2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ3MjAwNjgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZrNjP3e9iPPxJkJIXJuFlDmMTGA9is_4ZStwtIPg4T8)
### Are Subprime auto loans the reboot of 2008 no one wants?

| Oct 26

* * *
* * *
### The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication
### Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/8b1ed4cd-3d49-465d-ab44-1aa880152062?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
In the sun-baked lots of Dallas, where the air shimmers off rows of gleaming used sedans, Tricolor Auto Acceptance once promised a path to mobility for the overlooked. Founded in 2007 as a "buy-here-pay-here" lender targeting **[Hispanic and immigrant communities with thin credit files,](https://substack.com/redirect/cf172409-513f-4a2a-b3c7-79ebd2bbe297?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the company blended dealership sales with in-house financing, extending loans to those who banks deemed too risky. It was a model born of inclusion--or so it seemed.
By 2024, Tricolor had originated nearly **[$1 billion in subprime auto loans annually](https://substack.com/redirect/ff68ac97-36e9-4f5f-ae2e-4e6365c9ec2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , its **[AI-driven assessments](https://substack.com/redirect/4c3ebb5d-55cc-4109-80b6-2555ed48f867?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** touted as a democratizing force in a market where traditional lenders **[recoiled from the subprime fray.](https://substack.com/redirect/4c3ebb5d-55cc-4109-80b6-2555ed48f867?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** But then the facade crumbled. Allegations of fraud--double-pledged collateral, doctored loan portfolio documentation, loans made to illegal immigrants without an income or even a drivers license termed **["social lending"](https://substack.com/redirect/4c3ebb5d-55cc-4109-80b6-2555ed48f867?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**--led to a **[Chapter 7 bankruptcy](https://substack.com/redirect/03f35c15-c886-4f99-8aa9-c15d8ec9707c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** filing in Texas, listing $1 billion to $10 billion in **[assets and liabilities](https://substack.com/redirect/1ebce580-6e72-4988-bf3b-2e3c5441b483?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , ensnaring over 25,000 creditors.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9f68ac63-ed47-4316-b166-4defe3dbb8a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Fifth Third Bancorp alone booked a $178 million loss on a **[$200 million warehouse loan,](https://substack.com/redirect/10e68209-1b1f-417c-aa70-6e7ddd688b3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while JPMorgan Chase absorbed $170 million in **[charge-offs](https://substack.com/redirect/2a46797f-f343-4c08-9725-51119d654582?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. The trustee's report hinted at "**[systemic levels of fraud,](https://substack.com/redirect/6f62cf9a-dc5f-425c-95b9-098123b097b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** " a phrase that echoed through boardrooms like a long distant memory of a thunderclap from 17 years ago.
This is not an isolated tremor. Across the sprawl of America's used-car ecosystem, private credit--the shadowy counterpart to public bonds and bank loans--has woven itself into the fabric of everyday aspiration. A very visible sign of this is the massive increase in "Buy now, Pay later" companies such as Affirm and Klarna. What began as a niche for distressed borrowers now underpins a $3 trillion market, ballooning from $2 trillion in 2020 amid post-pandemic fervor and **[regulatory squeezes on banks.](https://substack.com/redirect/619e0d95-428a-40fb-8d65-affd52440347?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* * *
* * *
**[Sponsored By Vision Breakthrough](https://substack.com/redirect/e1357a73-362d-4f79-a29e-b3fea5654f71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# [Restore your 20/20 vision naturally by do THIS every morning](https://substack.com/redirect/e1357a73-362d-4f79-a29e-b3fea5654f71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e1357a73-362d-4f79-a29e-b3fea5654f71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Click here to learn more](https://substack.com/redirect/e1357a73-362d-4f79-a29e-b3fea5654f71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
Yet as delinquencies climb and firms falter, a question is nagging: Is private credit inflating a bubble destined to burst in the nation's garages, dragging down the working families it claims to uplift? The answer lies not in alarmism, but in the quiet mechanics of a system that trades speed for scrutiny, yield for opacity.
Private credit diverges sharply from the staid world of bank lending. Banks, bound by federal oversight like the Dodd-Frank Act, must hold substantial capital reserves against loans--often 8% or more of the principal--**[to buffer against defaults.](https://substack.com/redirect/62a3dfbc-4605-4c8b-ac5d-79bcbba9cf58?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Their processes are deliberate: rigorous credit checks, collateral appraisals, and public disclosures that invite market discipline. Private credit, by contrast, operates in the ether of direct negotiations between non-bank lenders--think hedge funds, pension pools, or boutique firms like Apollo Global Management--and borrowers.
These deals are bespoke, often senior secured loans, (they have the first in line for repayment over other debt, and secured against specific collateral like real estate or equipment) with floating rates - tied to benchmarks like the Secured Overnight Financing Rate set by the Treasury, and promising yields of 10% to 12% in a[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/bd73c30c-cbc5-4c72-8298-57c4b45fac4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[low-rate era](https://substack.com/redirect/bd73c30c-cbc5-4c72-8298-57c4b45fac4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
* **No public filings.**
* **No SEC scrutiny.**
* **Just covenants buried in confidential term sheets.**
* **They 're pay day loans for companies.**

Companies flock to this arena for its velocity. A midsize auto supplier, starved for inventory amid supply-chain snarls, can secure $500 million overnight without the bureaucratic drag of going through due diligence at a bank.
That due diligence exists for a reason. The **[Basel III reforms](https://substack.com/redirect/65e22c89-12cc-4f34-a861-3362ba373056?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **instituted in 2012, hiked the amount of money banks were required to hold in relation to riskier loans. The intention was to make sure that nothing like 2008's subprime mortgage explosion could ever happen again. As a result of the regulations, subprime plays by banks were no longer profitable so they largely exited that game. But just because banks couldn't play the subprime game anymore didn't mean the subprime game vanished, it simply left a void, a void that Private credit swept in to fill.
In 2025, as the Federal Reserve's stubbornness to lower rates bit into consumer wallets, consequently private credit's share of middle-market lending hit 70%, **[per Preqin data](https://substack.com/redirect/e2a2fefb-4771-4f39-8af9-1260b563ad0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. It's efficient, yes--but that efficiency masks perils. Without transparency, lenders chase volume over vetting, fostering "zombie firms" that limp along on an evergreen cycle of recirculating debt. Borrowers, meanwhile, leverage up in secrecy, their balance sheets become a black box with a faint ticking sound coming from inside it until insolvency strikes
No sector exemplifies this tension like used cars, where private credit has become the lifeblood--and potential hemlock--of a **[$500 billion annual market](https://substack.com/redirect/5af5507b-92ff-45a8-a144-ea641e2b45a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Why the entanglement? The used-car trade thrives on subprime borrowers: 55% of purchases are financed, with average FICO scores dipping below 600 for many, **[per Experian](https://substack.com/redirect/3d2b28b1-aed9-48ea-8470-f93eeac3a256?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. New cars, buoyed by manufacturer incentives, draw prime credit from captive lenders like Toyota Financial.
But used vehicles--affordable relics of pandemic-era price surges--lure the credit-impaired, who face APRs **[north of 20%](https://substack.com/redirect/501b8bb8-81ad-479c-9acc-83c2aa4e010d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Private credit steps in because banks, wary of repossession logistics and regulatory heat, ceded this turf.
Firms then bundle these loans into asset-backed securities (ABS), slicing them into tranches where the riskiest absorb losses first.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/770c8545-276c-4072-b66a-a67b0aef674d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In 2024, subprime auto ABS issuance topped $25 billion, much of it privately placed, as lenders bet on repossessable collateral: **[the cars themselves.](https://substack.com/redirect/ab864157-0c3d-4741-9cec-401f4d3207c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
This symbiosis has supercharged growth but sown vulnerabilities. Used-car prices, still 30% above pre-2020 levels **[per Kelley Blue Book](https://substack.com/redirect/a103537d-894b-4eb9-b144-3cdddcae0eae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , amplify loan-to-value ratios, turning minor delinquencies into cascades. Private credit's opacity exacerbates the issue: Investors in these ABS often lack granular data on borrower profiles or servicing quality. When inflation eroded savings--household financial fragility hit 40% in a 2024 **[Federal Reserve survey](https://substack.com/redirect/3e4a114b-b0e4-44d6-962b-e48763402856?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --the strain rippled upward.
Repossessions surged 20% year-over-year, **[per Cox Automotive](https://substack.com/redirect/7e1d1f63-7532-40ef-a38f-47d29af9e7c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , flooding lots with inventory and depressing values. It's a feedback loop: Cheaper used cars should ease access, but eroded collateral erodes lender confidence, hiking rates and tightening credit further.
The wreckage is mounting. Tricolor's implosion was merely the prelude. First Brands Group, an Ohio-based auto-parts maker, filed for Chapter 11 in late September, unveiling a $10 billion (at minimum) debt bomb **[laced with private credit](https://substack.com/redirect/99c33f13-ab2a-4629-8816-c89055d1dbfd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Acquired piecemeal through debt-fueled buys,--snapping up Autolite spark plugs and Raybestos brakes--First Brands debt total had ballooned, its $6 billion in junk bonds were merely the appetizer.
The real scandal however lurked "off-balance-sheet": Billions in undisclosed private loans, including $715 million from Jefferies' Point Bonita Capital secured against Walmart-bound inventory. Then it got worse. Creditors alleged $2.3 billion in assets "simply vanished," as assets that loans were secured against were discovered to have been pledged multiple times over to different lenders like UBS and Nomura. The CEO's **[abrupt resignation](https://substack.com/redirect/3d9461be-3639-4409-ad25-0bbf8ab1b01d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** amid accounting probes has ignited investigations, with total losses projected in the tens of billions--a stark rebuke to private credit's vaunted covenants.
Then came PrimaLend Capital Partners, the latest casualty. This subprime financier, armed buy-here-pay-here dealers with credit lines for sub-600 FICO buyers.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d6d65eeb-3acc-4f17-9325-c1adcf971af8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Apparently the "Strategic" lending "with tomorrow in mind" didn't reach to the day after tomorrow because PrimaLend defaulted on a $75 million bond in mid-2025. Months of creditor wrangling culminated in a Chapter 11 filing on October 22. as **[Bloomberg first reported.](https://substack.com/redirect/5b64b99d-8742-46c5-96cf-f8c192bddce2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/5b64b99d-8742-46c5-96cf-f8c192bddce2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)PrimaLend, which originated loans at 33% average APRs, blamed "industry-wide headwinds" like inflation and rate hikes for its crash.
Yet its portfolio--tied to the same distressed used-car chain--mirrored Tricolor's frailties: opaque servicing and an over reliance on ABS sales. With commitments for debtor-in-possession financing secured, it now eyes a sale, but the stain lingers. These collapses, clustered in auto's underbelly, signal not contagion yet, but contagion's preexisting conditions.
Wall Street's gaze now fixes on Carvana, the e-commerce wunderkind whose 70% stock surge in 2025 **[masks deepening fissures](https://substack.com/redirect/381ebd07-5ca1-48ba-997b-e72e9d767756?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Once a meme-stock phoenix, Carvana derives 26% of gross profits from subprime loan sales, bundling them into $851 million ABS pools **[peddled](https://substack.com/redirect/a56dedab-049e-40ae-8b4d-6a8af9c648cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to yield-hungry investors.
Delinquencies tell the tale of whats to come: 28.7% of its subprime loans are 30+ days past due, --four times the benchmark prime credit borrowers--**[and 12.7% are severely overdue](https://substack.com/redirect/7ae048f4-7b53-4f8c-8b72-d3e034ed6afe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Carvana loan extensions, doled out via DriveTime Automotive Group, a servicer run by **[CEO Ernie Garcia III's father](https://substack.com/redirect/3c5a049c-ef90-4a35-bbc6-62730db927b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**, have ballooned, delaying defaults but inflating principal. Net debt hovers at $4.8 billion, 4.4 times EBITDA, with $215 million in annual cash interest kicking **[in February.](https://substack.com/redirect/a56dedab-049e-40ae-8b4d-6a8af9c648cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Hindenburg Research likens it to pre-2008 grift, citing governance red flags inside the company and Ally Financial's $4 billion purchase commitment expiring **[January 2025.](https://substack.com/redirect/a56dedab-049e-40ae-8b4d-6a8af9c648cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon, stung by Tricolor, dubbed such risks "**[cockroaches](https://substack.com/redirect/22c3eb4e-80d5-4d15-9bb0-df4ee07a5886?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** ": hidden until they swarm. Carvana insists tighter underwriting has stemmed the bleed--2025 sales show just 5-10% delinquencies--but in a wider economic downturn, its model could vaporize **[overnight.](https://substack.com/redirect/f1ba8b73-9d9e-44b0-a6d1-cfb6e4606fc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/369800f7-b224-4e08-bb21-795febc2d301?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Averting an industry wide meltdown demands reckoning with private credit's Gordian knots. Foremost is opacity: Unlike public markets, where bond prices signal distress, private deals evade real-time pricing, breeding "evergreening." Where companies in distress are always available to find loans regardless of how bad their financials are (Tricolor, First Brands) because there is always someone willing to loan them money for a high yield. The company becomes a zombie, surviving on a diet of constant debt and so long as the company doesn't implode, the gravy train keeps running, but the implosions have begun to start.
Regulators like the SEC eye disclosure mandates, but enforcement lags; the $3 trillion pool dwarfs oversight capacity. Banks, dipping toes in via partnerships, face capital rules that deter deep dives, while pensions--holding 40% of these ABS assets, per Moody's--chase yields above all else, blind to tail risks and what sinister horror dwells in the deep.
Intervention risks moral hazard: Bailouts echo 2008, yet inaction invites spillovers to Collateralized Loan Obligations (a type of bond made up of lots of corporate bonds bundled together) and leveraged loans, and all are interconnected via $1.7 trillion in auto debt.
The IMF warns of correlated shocks to come; a used-car rout could idle factories and spike **[unemployment in rust-belt towns.](https://substack.com/redirect/5e34209e-80bf-4bf1-9b08-58789d524fb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Policymakers might hike transparency via regulation, implementing European style Alternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (AIFMD) rules in the U.S., but regulation may not have the intended outcome. After all, the regulations to prevent banks dealing in subprime loans have singularly failed to achieve a safer financial world, they simply pushed subprime debts in to a world with even less oversight than they had before.
Also, as Dimon warned, where there is one cockroach there are more. Because Private credit and shadow banking is an opaque market, no one knows how many zombie firms are out there surviving on a diet of evergreening debt. Were it to dry up how many of those firms could withstand going cold turkey?
In the end, private credit's used-car fixation reveals a deeper unease: an economy where mobility hinges on credit's precarious scaffolding. As credit yields seduce institutions and startups alike, Tricolor borrowers, many of them low income, chasing the American dream, now face repossession's sting, their vehicles--lifelines to their jobs--towed away.
For private credit the garage lights are flickering, the engines are still humming--for now, but did shadow banking fortify resilience or merely postpone fragility?
People were caught off guard by 2008, they couldn't figure out what happened and why there were no warning signs. 2008 could be about to get a Hollywood remake and for anyone who wants a warning sign this time, Tricolor, First Brands and PrimaLend is the fender bender.

The 8 lane multi car pileup could be coming?
* * *
* * *

Tricolor's implosion was merely the prelude. First Brands Group, an Ohio-based auto-parts maker, filed for Chapter 11 in late September, unveiling a $10 billion (at minimum) debt bomb . Acquired piecemeal through debt-fueled buys,--snapping up Autolite spark plugs and Raybestos brakes--First Brands debt total had ballooned, its $6 billion in junk bonds were merely the appetizer. The real scandal however lurked "off-balance-sheet": Billions in undisclosed private loans, including $715 million from Jefferies' Point Bonita Capital secured against Walmart-bound inventory. Then it got worse. Creditors alleged $2.3 billion in assets "simply vanished," as assets that loans were secured against were discovered to have been pledged multiple times over to different lenders like UBS and Nomura. The CEO's amid accounting probes has ignited investigations, with total losses projected in the tens of billions--a stark rebuke to private credit's vaunted covenants. Then came PrimaLend Capital Partners, the latest casualty. This subprime financier, armed buy-here-pay-here dealers with credit lines for sub-600 FICO buyers. | | ---|---|--- Apparently the "Strategic" lending "with tomorrow in mind" didn't reach to the day after tomorrow because PrimaLend defaulted on a $75 million bond in mid-2025. Months of creditor wrangling culminated in a Chapter 11 filing on October 22. as , which originated loans at 33% average APRs, blamed "industry-wide headwinds" like inflation and rate hikes for its crash. Yet its portfolio--tied to the same distressed used-car chain--mirrored Tricolor's frailties: opaque servicing and an over reliance on ABS sales. With commitments for debtor-in-possession financing secured, it now eyes a sale, but the stain lingers. These collapses, clustered in auto's underbelly, signal not contagion yet, but contagion's preexisting conditions. Wall Street's gaze now fixes on Carvana, the e-commerce wunderkind whose 70% stock surge in 2025 . Once a meme-stock phoenix, Carvana derives 26% of gross profits from subprime loan sales, bundling them into $851 million ABS pools to yield-hungry investors. Delinquencies tell the tale of whats to come: 28.7% of its subprime loans are 30+ days past due, --four times the benchmark prime credit borrowers--. Carvana loan extensions, doled out via DriveTime Automotive Group, a servicer run by , have ballooned, delaying defaults but inflating principal. Net debt hovers at $4.8 billion, 4.4 times EBITDA, with $215 million in annual cash interest kicking Hindenburg Research likens it to pre-2008 grift, citing governance red flags inside the company and Ally Financial's $4 billion purchase commitment expiring JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon, stung by Tricolor, dubbed such risks " ": hidden until they swarm. Carvana insists tighter underwriting has stemmed the bleed--2025 sales show just 5-10% delinquencies--but in a wider economic downturn, its model could vaporize | | ---|---|--- Averting an industry wide meltdown demands reckoning with private credit's Gordian knots. Foremost is opacity: Unlike public markets, where bond prices signal distress, private deals evade real-time pricing, breeding "evergreening." Where companies in distress are always available to find loans regardless of how bad their financials are (Tricolor, First Brands) because there is always someone willing to loan them money for a high yield. The company becomes a zombie, surviving on a diet of constant debt and so long as the company doesn't implode, the gravy train keeps running, but the implosions have begun to start. Regulators like the SEC eye disclosure mandates, but enforcement lags; the $3 trillion pool dwarfs oversight capacity. Banks, dipping toes in via partnerships, face capital rules that deter deep dives, while pensions--holding 40% of these ABS assets, per Moody's--chase yields above all else, blind to tail risks and what sinister horror dwells in the deep. Intervention risks moral hazard: Bailouts echo 2008, yet inaction invites spillovers to Collateralized Loan Obligations (a type of bond made up of lots of corporate bonds bundled together) and leveraged loans, and all are interconnected via $1.7 trillion in auto debt. The IMF warns of correlated shocks to come; a used-car rout could idle factories and spike Policymakers might hike transparency via regulation, implementing European style Alternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (AIFMD) rules in the U.S., but regulation may not have the intended outcome. After all, the regulations to prevent banks dealing in subprime loans have singularly failed to achieve a safer financial world, they simply pushed subprime debts in to a world with even less oversight than they had before. Also, as Dimon warned, where there is one cockroach there are more. Because Private credit and shadow banking is an opaque market, no one knows how many zombie firms are out there surviving on a diet of evergreening debt. Were it to dry up how many of those firms could withstand going cold turkey? In the end, private credit's used-car fixation reveals a deeper unease: an economy where mobility hinges on credit's precarious scaffolding. As credit yields seduce institutions and startups alike, Tricolor borrowers, many of them low income, chasing the American dream, now face repossession's sting, their vehicles--lifelines to their jobs--towed away. For private credit the garage lights are flickering, the engines are still humming--for now, but did shadow banking fortify resilience or merely postpone fragility? People were caught off guard by 2008, they couldn't figure out what happened and why there were no warning signs. 2008 could be about to get a Hollywood remake and for anyone who wants a warning sign this time, Tricolor, First Brands and PrimaLend is the fender bender. The 8 lane multi car pileup could be coming? The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matthew Miller 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/efe9d432-ea33-4d10-aa45-191b7b52bada?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Dominik Vanyi](https://substack.com/redirect/c5bc12e6-ecd9-487d-89ba-538899b1d03f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/5866dfad-b204-4d5e-8ffe-2b7fad0a4eba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Bitcoin runs on a simple idea: if telling a lie is expensive and telling the truth is cheap, most people will tell the truth. "Proof of Work" is how Bitcoin makes that happen.
Think of Bitcoin as a public notebook. When people make payments, a new page--called a "block"--gets added to the notebook. To add that page, volunteers called "miners" run machines that search for a special answer. It's not clever; it's persistent. The first miner to find the answer earns the right to add the page and gets paid in Bitcoin.
Why make it hard? Because the cost is the lock. If changing history were easy, crooks could erase or rewrite payments. With Proof of Work, attacking the record would require huge amounts of hardware and electricity--far more than an attacker could profit from. Honest behavior is cheaper than cheating. That's the whole point.
Everyone can check the work. Thousands of home and business computers, called "nodes," verify every rule: the signatures, the amounts, and the 21-million cap. If a miner tries to sneak in a bad page, nodes refuse it. The miner eats the electric bill and earns nothing. Miners propose; nodes dispose.
What about the power used? Energy isn't a bug; it's the security budget. It turns empty promises into real-world cost. And because miners pay their own bills, they hunt the cheapest energy on earth--often power that would be wasted, off-peak, or stranded. No government program required; prices do the steering.
Some prefer "Proof of Stake," where big coin holders help order the pages. It uses less electricity, but it ties control to wealth and social influence. Proof of Work ties security to physics. To change the rules, you don't lobby; you'd have to outspend the world on electricity and still persuade independent nodes. Good luck.
Here's the heart of it: rules beat rulers. Proof of Work is a neutral rule that treats everyone the same. It does not care who you are, who you know, or how you vote. It cares that you did the work, and that your page follows the rules. That's why it's still the best way to keep a money system honest in a noisy, digital world.
Real cost. Real limits. Real property rights. That's Proof of Work.

body:
These races will define what happens in 2026

| Friend,

**With Election Day just 9 days away, we need all hands on deck to ensure that Democrats win the critical gubernatorial races in New Jersey and Virginia.**

Both of these races are gaining national attention and getting more competitive with every day that passes – _meaning that if you’ve been looking for a time to jump in, this is it!_

These races are more than just state elections, they’re a barometer for next year’s midterms. A strong showing now can swing momentum toward Democrats and send a clear message: Americans reject the MAGA agenda.

As Donald Trump and his Republican allies push extremist policies — _from cutting Medicaid and Social Security to undermining our Constitutional institutions_ — **it’s critical that we elect Democratic governors who will protect our values and defend working families.**

That’s why we’re asking our top supporters to step up and help us reach voters directly about why it's critical we elect Mikie Sherrill and Abigail Spanberger. clean_body: These races will define what happens in 2026 | Friend, With Election Day just 9 days away, we need all hands on deck to ensure that Democrats win the critical gubernatorial races in New Jersey and Virginia. Both of these races are gaining national attention and getting more competitive with every day that passes – meaning that if you’ve been looking for a time to jump in, this is it! These races are more than just state elections, they’re a barometer for next year’s midterms. A strong showing now can swing momentum toward Democrats and send a clear message: Americans reject the MAGA agenda. As Donald Trump and his Republican allies push extremist policies — from cutting Medicaid and Social Security to undermining our Constitutional institutions — it’s critical that we elect Democratic governors who will protect our values and defend working families. Because of them, we are tied in the polls and ahead of our projections in New Jersey, and this race is 100% winnable.

**Republicans are returning their mail in ballots at a higher rate than Democrats.** This is phenomenal news, but it’s not all good... Democrat voters have requested almost 3 TIMES as many mail-in ballots as Republicans, and they still have more votes “in the bank” than we do.
Just like in Pennsylvania in 2024 for President Trump, this year in New Jersey **_we need to hit our magic number of mail-in and early in person Republican votes cast before election day_** so we can turn out the rest of our voters and win a decisive victory on Election Day.
We truly don’t have a moment to waste... in-person Early Voting begins on October 25th, mail-in voting ends on October 28th, and Election Day is November 4th.

Fellow Patriot, the momentum is on our side, and the early returns are looking very good, but we have a lot of work to do in the next few weeks if we’re going to flip the Garden State red.

name: Michael Bennet
subject: Colorado needs real climate leadership
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
It's everyday working Coloradans that are paying the price.
| | |
---
Friend,
Donald Trump and Washington Republicans are free to deny the reality of climate change and shield Big Polluters from accountability. They can try to maximize their corporate donors' profits by rolling back crucial regulations. But the truth remains: they are actively trying to gut the hundreds of billions in investments I helped pass in the Senate – investments designed to curb emissions by 40% by 2030 and create good-paying jobs that will drive our state's clean energy transition.
**And it's everyday working Coloradans that are paying the price.**
The American West is at its driest in nearly 1,200 years – and all summer long, we saw drought conditions persist across southwest Colorado.
So when the region saw one-third of its average annual rainfall this month, the dry ground turned this heavy fall rainstorm into a once-in-a-generation disaster.
The San Juan River crested at 12 feet. The Animas rose more than three feet in a single day. Hundreds of folks in La Plata and Archuleta Counties had to evacuate. _Thank God_ there were no casualties.
**But it's clear that climate change isn't waiting for D.C. Republicans, so neither can we. **
As the threats posed by climate change grow more urgent every day, Colorado needs a leader who will act with the same urgency to ensure the resilience of our communities. **_That's why I'm running._**
I'm running to invest in clean energy infrastructure and jobs that will build up Colorado's middle class and reduce working families' utility bills.
I'm running to safeguard Colorado's clean water, clean air, wildlife habitats and outdoor beauty – for generations to come – from Trump's reckless, climate-denying policies.
**I'm running because I know that together, we _can_ secure meaningful climate solutions that'll make Colorado the safest state to live and raise a family – but to make that happen, I need you on this grassroots team.**

clean_body: It's everyday working Coloradans that are paying the price. | | | Friend, Donald Trump and Washington Republicans are free to deny the reality of climate change and shield Big Polluters from accountability. They can try to maximize their corporate donors' profits by rolling back crucial regulations. But the truth remains: they are actively trying to gut the hundreds of billions in investments I helped pass in the Senate – investments designed to curb emissions by 40% by 2030 and create good-paying jobs that will drive our state's clean energy transition. And it's everyday working Coloradans that are paying the price. The American West is at its driest in nearly 1,200 years – and all summer long, we saw drought conditions persist across southwest Colorado. So when the region saw one-third of its average annual rainfall this month, the dry ground turned this heavy fall rainstorm into a once-in-a-generation disaster. The San Juan River crested at 12 feet. The Animas rose more than three feet in a single day. Hundreds of folks in La Plata and Archuleta Counties had to evacuate. Thank God there were no casualties. But it's clear that climate change isn't waiting for D.C. Republicans, so neither can we. As the threats posed by climate change grow more urgent every day, Colorado needs a leader who will act with the same urgency to ensure the resilience of our communities. That's why I'm running. I'm running to invest in clean energy infrastructure and jobs that will build up Colorado's middle class and reduce working families' utility bills. I'm running to safeguard Colorado's clean water, clean air, wildlife habitats and outdoor beauty – for generations to come – from Trump's reckless, climate-denying policies. I'm running because I know that together, we can secure meaningful climate solutions that'll make Colorado the safest state to live and raise a family – but to make that happen, I need you on this grassroots team. = | | = Thank you for reading and for everything you do, Michael | Bennet for Governor PO Box 270117 Louisville, CO 80027 United States Paid for by Bennet for Governor. ______________________________________| | | Peter, Jay Jones is making history, but for all the wrong reasons.

**Jones is actively under criminal investigation by a special prosecutor **— and he's running to be Virginia's top law enforcement officer. This has **never** happened in Virginia's history.

Think about that for a moment. **The man who wants the power to prosecute your family and our police is currently being investigated himself.**

---
A special prosecutor doesn't get appointed for routine cases, friend. **This means there are serious issues involved, and the investigation is "currently pending."**

**You cannot be Virginia’s Attorney General while being investigated by Virginia’s criminal prosecutors. This is insanity.**
---
[ **STOP THIS MADNESS** ](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=0ef95b58ec7b4b0e991177c857b814b1&_e=PaMYvOSvvLNIs_tR9ZkopJT_fIzmDFcvU7Qi6xd99uLjq9gtfiazxuEd_O1oU6nial_HvYcqz958D4r0Tbs7D6KDY8mJnoa4x13MwEH2CyquQ_iICB79A2A24sE-AMLWpoRyKxH8gMwImy_1NeS7cZ9ria-Bonmd_7IUksmhZO-rsrRHRLSbke8GVGhHnMWiAnC5wrX-C5xXJb6ZB9ctkqwYPTjo-SuWOdzE57Mxe0Bm2A_AP1BZ8WEsCvJW0MnQ44FfUDdTLwicRoeZvkIzCJ71ApU6odz8ZqsTrs2PAZKC3D4oOvbodkWqyLgIf78p0YQ_cUcyh4gpYt-J08wrMGHsreuoc47OgaLGIOqUrPuSy_TgQFKGgAlyO6MLYhCnLQqPTsJmA3UC1021oM5oVNg7SvUGo1iNR1JPC-6sepXdYkZpzk39nVFp8Adx0qBxHS2Ph_t-xgUocwQWbVGnJarbtXFa69mgiV0_vGLbAmtlKl_EG4l3haToYLuFuLFfTxpdts1JJZUo231B8GeUeP-6_DzzHvD0z9RpbaUtCOsUFuTlBAoF6HRpvFZV9FXTjvnpn1uiEhqlHKjR16sgw79baTwl3KDqqYmmrkpSOMH9CaXxVPmDa-DL_ttukUsy)
---
As a prosecutor who's spent years working with other prosecutors across Virginia, I can tell you this is unprecedented. **No** credible law enforcement professional would hire someone under active criminal investigation — let alone make them their boss.

Jones wants to lead the very system that's currently investigating him for criminal conduct. **That's not just wrong — it's the recipe for a constitutional crisis in Virginia.**

Jay Jones should suspend his campaign and withdraw from the race for Attorney General.

⚠️ _**[Stand with me to stop this madness before it’s too late.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=0ef95b58ec7b4b0e991177c857b814b1&_e=PaMYvOSvvLNIs_tR9ZkopJT_fIzmDFcvU7Qi6xd99uLjq9gtfiazxuEd_O1oU6nial_HvYcqz958D4r0Tbs7D6KDY8mJnoa4x13MwEH2CyquQ_iICB79A2A24sE-AMLWpoRyKxH8gMwImy_1NeS7cZ9ria-Bonmd_7IUksmhZO-rsrRHRLSbke8GVGhHnMWiAnC5wrX-C5xXJb6ZB9ctkpaj4JjDblvLJh5UTMWLCvsInw0E0q8YZVh9C6JPehxA9kHMj3_TmbuD4VNf8bsQTLfk4Zx0-8WbMtO0OQQh1cD6VyPzijarCxYgb-V0eZzqlywYU_KScLt2YfTo5WQewi0tBOWMbwk8ObAaHsinqdCKfP_pIVIS5xK6H6ceVK_gtw_TD0WpdKF5y6nILDYSOw3QvSObhDsbagR4sBkVFwAz7g3fAMVK0Fhb2p7RGvSnSMfepXhMGPPSplNUh1QidBmkcfrA-IEynMz8HbrWbWXiv9xFh7XMsbn8ROV6Im4Y73xQ687Nwmo8MpvX8dBJvUbvWFubbeamiVjf5DHorD8doEHPl3f9lssNRnu0W018GEjmXPYjssSHR7URbt6zTIwR8iwqxXiXHvYe-uBJ5wG1-6AsFWfYbhcWmpe1ZE6Z)**_

name: Chris Pappas HQ
subject: Further proof we’re in for a tough fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
We’re fighting back.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)
National Republicans are lining up behind John Sununu. [**Rush a donation right now to help us fight back.**](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251026_1_nrsc.DYWZq1&refcode=em20251026-1&t=2&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)
This week the NRSC and Senate Leadership Fund, two national Republican committees determined to flip New Hampshire, said they are ALL-IN to support John Sununu’s campaign launch.
**There’s no denying we’re in for a tough fight ahead.**
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251026_1_nrsc.DYWZq1&refcode=em20251026-1&t=3&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)
These groups could pour millions into New Hampshire, making an already competitive race even more difficult, and political reporters are predicting that this race could break NH records. Republicans have been trying to recruit Sununu for _months_ , and they finally succeeded. Now we need to double down on our efforts to get Chris Pappas elected so we can defend _all_ Granite Staters.
This race is going to be one of the most difficult in the country, so your support is more critical than ever.
[**Please, donate any amount you can today so we can stay competitive and have a chance to send Chris Pappas to the U.S. Senate.**](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251026_1_nrsc.DYWZq1&refcode=em20251026-1&t=4&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251026_1_nrsc.DYWZq1&amount=5&refcode=em20251026-1&t=23&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)**
Thank you for all that you do,
Chris Pappas HQ
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251026-1&t=24&refcode2=3114_582294_arU7kP&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=25&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=26&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=3114%2E582294%2EarU7kP)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)
Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
Chris Pappas for Senate
PO Box 313
Manchester, NH 03105
United States

name: Deb Haaland
subject: A feeling I’ll never forget
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
The anxiety of having to choose between feeding your child or making rent is a feeling you never forget.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=5256%2E400544%2EH4X1Cq)
When it comes to the struggles facing working people across our country, **one of the most personal to me is making sure families can put food on the table.**
My heart breaks each time I hear from someone worried about paying for groceries as prices rise, or workers not getting a paycheck because of the government shutdown. It brings me back to a time in my life when I relied on food stamps to feed my child, Somáh, and myself.
**As a single mom working my way through law school, tortillas and pinto beans became our dinnertime staples.** That combo sustained us through some pretty lean years until one day, the week of Thanksgiving, I found myself staring at a near-empty kitchen cabinet.
I desperately hoped to cook Somáh a big holiday meal, so I went to apply for food stamps. My application was denied, and I broke down and cried right there in the office. It wasn’t until later on, when Somáh and I were couch surfing among friends to keep a roof over our heads, that we finally became eligible for the food assistance we so urgently needed.
When I meet working parents on the campaign trail who tell me they’re struggling to keep up with rising prices, I think back to those late nights when I’d sift through the bargain carts at my local grocery store. **The anxiety of having to choose between feeding your child or making rent is a feeling you never really forget – and it’s what motivates me to fight relentlessly to keep New Mexico affordable for working families.**
**[I understand the challenges hardworking New Mexicans are facing because I’ve lived them, and the determination that comes from tough times drives my leadership. If your budget allows for it, I hope you’ll chip in $5 to grow our grassroots campaign.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251026_1_pintobeans.wxmTFQ&amount=5&refcode=em20251026-5256&t=2&akid=5256%2E400544%2EH4X1Cq)**
I have a long list of priorities I’d like to tackle as governor, but at the top of that list is making sure our neighbors aren't going hungry because they can't afford to buy food.
Donald Trump and ultra-rich billionaires are wreaking havoc on our country and putting vital social programs on the chopping block. Trump’s tariffs have raised prices. **Now, food stamp benefits, that thousands of families in New Mexico rely on to feed their children, may not be issued on November 1 because Trump shut down the government.**
Our state deserves a leader who will prioritize bringing costs down and protect the programs that help families survive, not sell out New Mexico families to pad the pockets of billionaire donors.
**[If you can contribute $5 today, I’d be so grateful to help us build a winning campaign. But if that’s not something you can afford right now, just know I’m deeply thankful to have you as part of this team.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251026_1_pintobeans.wxmTFQ&amount=5&refcode=em20251026-5256&t=3&akid=5256%2E400544%2EH4X1Cq)**
Thanks for all you do,
Deb
P.S. Twenty-something years later, Somáh and I still make time to bond over a simple dinner of handmade tortillas and pinto beans. 💛
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20251026-1&t=4&refcode2=5256_400544_H4X1Cq&akid=5256%2E400544%2EH4X1Cq)**
[](https://deb4gov.us/text-me)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=5&akid=5256%2E400544%2EH4X1Cq)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=6&akid=5256%2E400544%2EH4X1Cq)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)

subject: In 9 days, the fate of Democracy will be decided in PA
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
Now with only 9 days to go, we are preparing our final surge—one last hail mary to try and prevent MAGA from seizing the court and Democracy from falling:
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Zb0SMA7QhgR25rfvdRIn8Ri2Qxk8JL3XXrTMFoVDrpTjQXf68zX3ikIswxdDM3llbdSok-8zlxOF_V3HqqswBkpaiGiZphPX4EVEBCJsNhrFkbPjbQxmDT-s2oPlfKuL0Ojr50T-WrkGgtiLeyCIDx-GXVe0WRobFlaMNXM_Ijd3T06D0d5Owc4kRIcR8eQQCYZ7ebs_xNCJT-s847Lqgrea-XrD-dHyypkxwMxXwtg=&c=bNPzHlP6OESF93TZHIM4qNFT_sDIZUR20luU6X-xxAVDwDBjDJRc5g==&ch=mv1D-CXdHwU4soKwpC_kd_4AflVfc9-QfdecxV5m3LrUvkrj0E2tpw==)
---
| Peter,
There are now only 9 days left until the most critical election of 2025 for Democrats--the November 4th PA Supreme Court retention election.
The stakes of this race are existential for Democrats.
If MAGA and Trump succeed in toppling the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court, they will gain control over the court responsible for how elections are run in the most important swing state in America. This will allow Trump to rig the upcoming '26 & '28 elections--denying Democrats a midterm victory and control of the White House for at least a decade.
Since earlier this summer, Defend Our Courts has been one of the leading Democratic organizations spearheading the defense of this critical court, and we have since rallied tens of thousands of Democrats to come to PA's aid.
Now with only 9 days to go, we are preparing our final surge--one last hail mary to try and prevent MAGA from seizing the court and Democracy from falling:
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Zb0SMA7QhgR25rfvdRIn8Ri2Qxk8JL3XXrTMFoVDrpTjQXf68zX3ikIswxdDM3llbdSok-8zlxOF_V3HqqswBkpaiGiZphPX4EVEBCJsNhrFkbPjbQxmDT-s2oPlfKuL0Ojr50T-WrkGgtiLeyCIDx-GXVe0WRobFlaMNXM_Ijd3T06D0d5Owc4kRIcR8eQQCYZ7ebs_xNCJT-s847Lqgrea-XrD-dHyzshisHFzgCE=&c=bNPzHlP6OESF93TZHIM4qNFT_sDIZUR20luU6X-xxAVDwDBjDJRc5g==&ch=mv1D-CXdHwU4soKwpC_kd_4AflVfc9-QfdecxV5m3LrUvkrj0E2tpw==)
---
| | | | Behind the scenes look at the filming of Defend Our Courts closing ads in this critical election.
Just a few days ago, our team wrapped up filming for our final slate of ads for this election. The ads feature Pennsylvanians speaking directly to their neighbors about why voting YES in the retention election matters -- from protecting voting rights and fair districts to defending fundamental freedoms.
The production team is working around the clock to finalize edits, polish color grading, sync sound, and master the ad so that we can roll out these ads out across PA in the final week.
That's why we've set a goal to raise $1 million before Tuesday -- enough to reach every targeted voter in Pennsylvania for less than $1.50 each, blanket the airwaves, and shut out MAGA's billionaire propaganda in the final stretch.
With over 50,000 Defend Our Courts supporters nationwide, if each supporter chipped in just $20, we'd have enough to fund every ad, reach every voter, and blanket Pennsylvania's airwaves before Election Day.
[Can we count on you to rush a donation of $20 or whatever you can spare to help Defend Our Courts blanket the airwaves in PA in the last week of the election and save this critical court?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Zb0SMA7QhgR25rfvdRIn8Ri2Qxk8JL3XXrTMFoVDrpTjQXf68zX3ikIswxdDM3ll0-nhslPqiFeSoHdqwejEXXSadHKeIJYKgUN2-pMG5k_pcKIUWWIpoaXLZWqji-gaDn0g2nf50mlTXOBceovTWhPJVVpSdp5wLlqkaFPDH8r5UD58Eier7GO0j2qITVWltaTQesilAYuCb37nMX1ng7PmJIEekEq2D1ZZIdiYa3wVLUt405FATA==&c=bNPzHlP6OESF93TZHIM4qNFT_sDIZUR20luU6X-xxAVDwDBjDJRc5g==&ch=mv1D-CXdHwU4soKwpC_kd_4AflVfc9-QfdecxV5m3LrUvkrj0E2tpw==)

name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
subject: The simple truth
body:
The radical Left is trying to CRUSH us.
| | | | Friend – Here are three simple truth:
The Left HATES me. The media lies about me. **The Uniparty wants me gone.**
**_Too bad. I’m not going anywhere._**
I’m not here to make friends in D.C.
I’m here to rip the mask off the corrupt, sellout cowards running this country into the ground.
**I’m here to FIGHT for the citizens of this country. Nobody else.**
Here’s another simple truth: **_[I need you with me](https://link.email.greene4congress.)_****.**
I’m staring down the end-of-month deadline and ALL of my enemies are watching my numbers to see if the grassroots patriots still have my back.
So I need to know, right now: **Do you?**
---
[DEFEND MTG >>>](https://link.email.greene4congress.)
If you want somebody who will **go along to get along,****_I’m not your person._**
If you want someone who will **toe the line, and not stir things up too much,****_that’s not me._**
But if you want somebody who is fighting every day against every ounce of corruption, and somebody who has looked the DC swamp in the eye and refused to blink... **__[Please, stand with me right now](https://link.email.greene4congress.)__**** _._**
[DEFEND MTG >>>](https://link.email.greene4congress.)
_In Strength,_
| | [](https://link.email.greene4congress.)
---
**Marjorie Taylor Greene**
[](https://link.email.greene4congress.)

### **_[Unsubscribe from list](https://link.email.greene4congress.com/click/e3707448/VaHR0cHM6Ly9tdGcubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfZ3JlZW5lRk9VUmNvbmdyZXNzX2NvbTI1MTAyNg/LbmNsYmtFQzZ3/qP2hpZHM9bmN4TFBoOUJoR2dhcGxz/gaP57fg/s5hf117d8db)_**

subject: This is the end
